subject: Chemistry
options: By using catalytic converters in the automobiles/industry
solution: Catalytic converters force <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>CO</mi></math> and incompletely combusted hydrocarbons to react with a metal catalyst, typically platinum, to produce <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>CO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo> </mo></math>and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></math>. Additionally, catalytic converters reduce nitrogen oxides from exhaust gases into <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>, eliminating the cycle of ozone formation.

solution: C2Cl2​F2​ is an example of freon. It can act as an aerosol propellant, refrigerant, or organic solvent because it is a gas at room temperature and a liquid when cooled or compressed. Freon is a term that was commonly used to refer to a group of chlorofluorocarbon (CFC) and hydrochlorofluorocarbon (HCFC) compounds.

solution: Clean water has a BOD value of less than 5 ppm whereas highly polluted water could have a BOD value of <math><mn>17</mn></math>ppm so more polluted.

options: Water has high amount of fluoride compounds
solution:
The amount of BOD in the water is a measure of the amount of organic material in the water, in terms of how much oxygen will be required to break it down biologically.

Clean water has BOD value of less than 5 ppm whereas polluted water has a BOD value of 17 ppm or more.

So, the pond has very clean water.

options: Large amount of water alone for washing clothes
Liquified <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>CO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> for dry cleaning of clothes
solution:
Chlorine gas was used earlier for bleaching paper. These days, hydrogen peroxide H2O2 is used with suitable catalyst.

Tetrachloroethene Cl2C=CCl2 was earlier used as solvent for dry cleaning. The compound contaminates the ground water and is also a suspected carcinogen. Replacement of halogenated solvent by liquid CO2 will result in less harm to groundwater. Hence, given statement (4) is correct.

subject: Chemistry
solution: The process in which nutrient enriched water bodies support a dense plant population which kills animal life by depriving it of oxygen and results in subsequent loss of biodiversity is known as eutrophication.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Smog is derived from smoke + fog, this is most common example of air pollution, photochemical smog occurs in warm, dry and sunny climate also known as oxidizing smog.
Unburnt hydrocarbons + Nitric oxide
NO2g→hνNOg+Og
O2g+Og→O3g
CH4+2O3→3CH2OFormaldehyde+3H2O
So the common component of photochemical smog are Ozone, No, acrolein, PAN, formaldehyde.

options: Both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>)</mo></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi><mo>)</mo></math> are true but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi><mo>)</mo></math> is not the true explanation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>)</mo></math>.

Both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>)</mo></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi><mo>)</mo></math> are true and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi><mo>)</mo></math> is the true explanation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>)</mo></math>.
solution:
Photochemical smog has high concentration of oxidising agents and is, therefore, called as oxidising smog.
The common components of photochemical smog are ozone, nitric oxide, acrolein, formaldehyde and peroxyacetyl nitrate (PAN). Photochemical smog causes serious health problems. Both ozone and PAN act as powerful eye irritants. Ozone and nitric oxide irritate the nose and throat and their high concentration causes headache, chest pain, dryness of the throat, cough and difficulty in breathing. Photochemical smog leads to cracking of rubber and extensive damage to plant life. It also causes corrosion of metals, stones, building materials, rubber and painted surfaces.

solution: Acid rain is harmful for trees and plants. It causes breathing problem in human being and animals.

subject: Chemistry
options: in the stratosphere, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>CFC</mi></math>s release chlorine free radicals <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi>CI</mi></mfenced></math> <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"></math>which reacts with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> to give chlorine
in the atmosphere, it is depleted by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>CFC</mi></math>s.
in the stratosphere, it forms a protective shield against UV radiation.

It is a toxic gas and its reaction with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>NO</mi></math> gives <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>NO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>
solution:
In presence of sunlight CFC’s molecule divides & release chlorine free radical, which react with ozone give chlorine monoxide radical (ClO) and oxygen

CF2Cl2g⟶UVC˙lg+C˙F2Clg

Cl*(g)+O3(g)⟶ClO*(g)+O2(g)

ClO∙(g)+O(g)⟶Cl'(g)+O2(g)

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Formula of ppm is-

ppm=weight of soluteweight of solution×106

ppm=10.3×10-31030×106 =10

PPM is a concentration term and its full form is "parts per million" and it also can be expressed as milligrams per liter (mg/L). This measurement is the mass of a chemical or contaminate per unit volume of water.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD) is also called Biological Oxygen Demand.

It is the amount of dissolved oxygen needed (i.e. demanded) by aerobic biological organisms to break down organic material present in a given water sample at certain temperature over a specific time period

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Acid rain is a by product of a variety of human activities that emit the oxides of sulphur and nitrogen in the atmosphere. As mentioned earlier, burning of fossil fuels (which contain sulphur and nitrogenous matter) such as coal and oil in power stations and furnaces or petrol and diesel in motor engines produce sulphur dioxide and nitrogen oxides. SO2 and NO2 after oxidation and reaction with water are major contributors to acid rain, because polluted air usually contains particulate matter that catalyse the oxidation.

2SO2g+O2g+2H2Ol→2H2SO4aq

4NO2g+O2g+2H2Ol→4HNO3aq

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Nitogen oxides are produced by various natural and human activities, including combustion processes, industrial emissions, and vehicle exhaust. These are responsible for acid rains. 

Methane (CH4) is a potent greenhouse gas that contributes to global warming. While carbon dioxide (CO2) is the most well-known greenhouse gas. Carbon dioxide present in air, react with the rain water to form carbonic acid.

The excessive use or runoff of phosphate fertilizers into bodies of water can contribute to eutrophication.

A Nitrogen oxides in air IV Acid rain
B Methane in air III Global warming
C Carbon dioxide II pH of rain water becomes 5.6
D Phosphate fertilisers in water I Eutrophication

∴ A-IV; B-III; C-II; D-I

subject: Chemistry
options: Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct
Both the statements I and II are incorrect
Both the statements I and II are correct
solution:
Drinking water has a BOD level of 1 - 2 ppm. When the BOD value of water is in the range 3 - 5 ppm, the water is moderately clean. Polluted water has a BOD value in the range of 6 - 9 ppm. Healthy water should generally have dissolved oxygen concentrations above 6.5-8 mg/L

Drinking water with concentrations of nitrate (measured as nitrate-nitrogen) below 10 mg of nitrate per liter of water (mg/L) is considered safe. Permissible limit of Zn in potable water is 5 ppm.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Rain water has a pH of  5-6 due to presence of H+ions formed by the reaction of rain water with carbon dioxide present in the atmosphere.

H2O(l)+CO2( g)⇌H2CO3

H2CO3⇌H++HCO3

So, the correct option is A.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
F- ion concentration above 2 ppm causes brown mottling of teeth. At the same time, excess fluoride (over 10 ppm) causes harmful effect to bones and teeth

Lead: Drinking water gets contaminated with lead when lead pipes are used for transportation of water. The prescribed upper limit concentration of lead in drinking water is about 50 ppb. Lead can damage kidney, liver, reproductive system etc.

Sulphate: Excessive sulphate >500 ppm in drinking water causes laxative effect, otherwise at moderate levels it is harmless.

Nitrate: The maximum limit of nitrate in drinking water is 50 ppm. Excess nitrate in drinking water can cause disease such as methaemoglobinaemia ('blue baby' syndrome).

subject: Chemistry
solution: On heating concentration of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> in water decreases. So boiling water and water at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>80</mn><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> having less <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> concentration. Polluted water also having less <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> concentration. So water at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> is having maximum <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> concentration.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
In presence of ozone O3, oxidising smog gets increased during the daytime because automobiles and factories produce main components of the photochemical smog (oxidising smog) results from the action of sunlight on unsaturated hydrocarbon and nitrogen oxide.

Ozone is a strong oxidising agent and can react with the unburnt hydrocarbons in the polluted air to produce chemicals.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The amount of oxygen required by bacteria to break down the organic matter present in a certain volume of a sample of water, is called Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD).

The amount of BOD in the water is a measure of the amount of organic material in the water, in terms of how much oxygen will be required to break it down biologically.

Clean water would have BOD value of less than 5 ppm whereas highly polluted water could have a BOD value of 17 ppm or more.

Hence,Among the given samples, sample number B and C are polluted.

H2O2 is used as bleaching agent in laundry.

subject: Chemistry
solution: The various solid wastes in the form of slags and sludges that are emerged from steel plants are blast furnace slag. Fly ash is a fine by-product recovered from gases of burning coal in thermal power plants. Gypsum is the waste of fertiliser industry. Non-cellulosic part of plant is wastes of paper industry and it is biodegradable.

subject: Chemistry
solution: CO2 is used in dry cleaning is a better strategy to control environmental pollution. Liquid carbon dioxide is better to replace conventional halogenated solvents which are potentially carcinogenic. These detergents are developed in a way that one end of the molecule is soluble in non-polar substances like grease, oil stains and the other end dissolves in liquid carbon dioxide.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Photochemical smog, also known as 'Oxidising smog' is an atmospheric condition that produces various unwanted effects such as eye irritation and poor visibility.

The main ingredients of photochemical smog include energy from sunlight, hydrocarbons and oxides of nitrogen.

The name 'Oxidising smog' is given because it contains high concentration of oxidising agents.

subject: Chemistry
options: Both Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></math> and Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>II</mi></math> are true.
Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></math> is false but Statement <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>II</mi></math> is true.

solution:
Both statements are correct. Normally rain water has pH of 5.6 due to the presence of H+ ions formed by the reaction of rain water with carbon dioxide present in the atmosphere.

Normal rain has a pH of about 5.6. This is because it reacts with carbon dioxide in the atmosphere and forms mildly acidic carbonic acid before it becomes rain.

subject: Chemistry
options: Polluted water has BOD value higher than $10 \mathrm{ppm}$
Aerobic bacteria decreases the BOD value
Anaerobic bacteria increases the BOD value
